The finishing happened at a named past point and is over, which makes it a bounded event rather than something still running. A bounded past event takes the passato prossimo. Finisco would put the finishing in the present, which 'ieri sera' flatly contradicts.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: Complete: 'Anna _____ in Francia per cinque anni, poi è tornata in Italia.' Choose: vive / ha vissuto.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo for a completed event (ha vissuto)

explanation: 'Anna ha vissuto in Francia per cinque anni, poi è tornata in Italia' means 'Anna lived in France for five years, then she came back to Italy.' The five years are over: she left. That makes the living a bounded event, so it takes the passato prossimo. The preposition is the whole diagnostic here: per cinque anni closes the stretch, where da cinque anni would have kept it open and required vive.
examiner_note: Same-surface pair with tc_pres_pp_da_08 (house technique 3): identical verb and supplied forms, opposite answers, conditioning factor da vs per. 'poi è tornata' closes the stretch beyond doubt per criterion 10a.

prompt: Complete: 'Io _____ l'italiano per due anni, poi ho smesso.' Choose: studio / ho studiato.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo for a completed event (ho studiato)

explanation: 'Ho studiato l'italiano per due anni, poi ho smesso' means 'I studied Italian for two years, then I stopped.' The stopping puts a wall at the end of the two years, so the studying is a bounded event and takes the passato prossimo. Per two years measures a finished block of time; da two years would have measured a block still running and forced studio instead.
examiner_note: Same-surface pair with tc_pres_pp_da_01 (house technique 3).

prompt: Complete: 'Noi _____ mezz'ora e poi siamo andati via.' Choose: aspettiamo / abbiamo aspettato.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo for a completed event (abbiamo aspettato)

explanation: 'Abbiamo aspettato mezz'ora e poi siamo andati via' means 'We waited half an hour and then we left.' Leaving ends the wait, so the waiting is a bounded event with both edges in the past, and that takes the passato prossimo. Compare the durative version: aspettiamo da mezz'ora would mean we are still standing there.
examiner_note: Same-surface pair with tc_pres_pp_da_03 (house technique 3).

prompt: Complete: 'Stamattina _____ l'autobus e sono arrivato tardi.' Choose: perdo / ho perso.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo for a completed event (ho perso)

explanation: 'Stamattina ho perso l'autobus e sono arrivato tardi' means 'This morning I missed the bus and I arrived late.' Missing the bus is a single, finished moment, which is a bounded event and takes the passato prossimo. Note that stamattina still counts as past even though the day is not over: what matters for a bounded event is that the moment itself has closed, not that the day has.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: Complete: 'L'anno scorso loro _____ in Sicilia.' Choose: vanno / sono andati.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo for a completed event (sono andati)

explanation: 'L'anno scorso sono andati in Sicilia' means 'Last year they went to Sicily.' The trip sits inside a closed past year, so it is a bounded event and takes the passato prossimo. L'anno scorso is a definite past-time marker, and a definite past-time marker is incompatible with vanno, which would place the trip in present time.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: Complete: 'Un attimo fa _____ tua madre.' Choose: telefona / ha telefonato.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo for a completed event (ha telefonato)

explanation: 'Un attimo fa ha telefonato tua madre' means 'Your mother called a moment ago.' The call is finished, so it is a bounded event and takes the passato prossimo, even though it happened seconds ago. Recency is not the test: an event a moment old is as closed as one a year old. The word doing the work is fa, which measures backwards from now to a finished point.
examiner_note: Criterion 18, third direction: 'telefona' is a substring of the correct 'ha telefonato', so the must_not_include entry is word-anchored to stop it firing a false diagnostic on a correct answer.

prompt: In un testo di storia: 'Dante _____ a Firenze nel 1265.' Choose: nacque / è nato.

markpoints.label: Passato remoto in historical narration (nacque)

explanation: 'Dante nacque a Firenze nel 1265' means 'Dante was born in Florence in 1265.' A history book is telling you about a life sealed off from the present, with no thread running from it to today. That is the job of the passato remoto: the narrative past of writing, history and literature. È nato is not ungrammatical, but it would sound like conversation rather than a history text, so the register cue decides it.
examiner_note: Register stated per criterion 11. Criterion 19 no-op: the accent-stripped twin of nacque is nacque itself (no accent), and in any case the candidate set here is {remoto, PP}, so no stripped present form is a candidate answer.

prompt: In un testo di storia: 'L'impero romano d'Occidente _____ nel 476.' Choose: cadde / è caduto.

markpoints.label: Passato remoto in historical narration (cadde)

explanation: 'L'impero romano d'Occidente cadde nel 476' means 'The Western Roman Empire fell in 476.' The event is finished, remote, and being recounted rather than reported, which is the home ground of the passato remoto. The remoto signals that the event is closed and detached from present relevance. È caduto would drag the fall towards the speaker's here-and-now, which a history text does not want.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: In stile narrativo: 'Il re _____ e il regno passò al figlio.' Choose: morì / è morto.

markpoints.label: Passato remoto in narration (morì)

explanation: 'Il re morì e il regno passò al figlio' means 'The king died and the kingdom passed to his son.' This is storytelling, and the passato remoto is the tense that carries a narrative forward event by event. Notice the second verb, passò: once a narrative is running in the remoto it stays there, so a passato prossimo dropped in the middle would break the register.
examiner_note: The adjacent 'passò' pins the register from inside the sentence as well as via the explicit cue, so the item is robust if a learner skims the cue.

prompt: In un testo di storia: 'Manzoni _____ i Promessi Sposi nel 1827.' Choose: scrisse / ha scritto.

markpoints.label: Passato remoto in historical narration (scrisse)

explanation: 'Manzoni scrisse i Promessi Sposi nel 1827' means 'Manzoni wrote The Betrothed in 1827.' A dated literary fact set down in a history of literature is exactly what the passato remoto is for. The remoto puts the writing in a closed past with no line drawn to the present. Ha scritto would work if you were chatting about Manzoni, but not in the register the cue names.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: In un testo di storia: 'Garibaldi _____ in Sicilia nel 1860.' Choose: sbarcò / è sbarcato.

markpoints.label: Passato remoto in historical narration (sbarcò)

explanation: 'Garibaldi sbarcò in Sicilia nel 1860' means 'Garibaldi landed in Sicily in 1860.' The landing is a dated, completed historical event being narrated, so it takes the passato remoto. Note that the remoto is a simple tense: it needs no auxiliary at all, where the passato prossimo of sbarcare would have to choose essere and agree the participle. That economy is part of why narrative prose prefers it.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: In stile narrativo: 'La porta si aprì ed _____ un uomo alto.' Choose: entrò / è entrato.

markpoints.label: Passato remoto in narration (entrò)

explanation: 'La porta si aprì ed entrò un uomo alto' means 'The door opened and a tall man came in.' There is no date here at all, and that is the point: what selects the passato remoto is the narrative register, not remoteness in years. The remoto is the tense of the story being told, so it fits a scene unfolding a second after the previous one. È entrato would sound like a witness statement rather than a novel.
examiner_note: Deliberately dateless, to break the pattern that remoto = old date. This is the one item in the leaf where register alone carries the diagnostic; the explicit cue keeps it criterion-11 compliant.

prompt: In un testo di storia: 'Leonardo _____ la Gioconda intorno al 1503.' Choose: dipinse / ha dipinto.

markpoints.label: Passato remoto in historical narration (dipinse)

explanation: 'Leonardo dipinse la Gioconda intorno al 1503' means 'Leonardo painted the Mona Lisa around 1503.' A dated fact in a history of art is narrated, not reported, so it takes the passato remoto. The painting still hangs in the Louvre, but present relevance of the object is not the test: what matters is that the act of painting is closed and is being recounted.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: In conversazione: 'Ieri _____ Marco al bar.' Choose: vidi / ho visto.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo in speech (ho visto)

explanation: 'Ieri ho visto Marco al bar' means 'Yesterday I saw Marco at the bar.' Standard spoken Italian reaches for the passato prossimo for past events, and a recent one told conversationally is its clearest case. Vidi is not wrong Italian, but in casual speech it lands as bookish, as though you were narrating your own life in a novel.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: In conversazione: 'Io _____ nel 1990.' Choose: nacqui / sono nato.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo in speech (sono nato)

explanation: 'Io sono nato nel 1990' means 'I was born in 1990.' The date is decades back, yet standard conversation still takes the passato prossimo, because the choice tracks register and present relevance rather than how long ago the event was. Your own birth is about as present-relevant as a fact gets: it is why you are here. Nacqui would be correct in a written biography of you, which is not what a conversation is.
examiner_note: Same-surface pair with tc_rem_pp_lit_01 (house technique 3): nascere in both leaves with opposite answers. The conditioning factor is register, not date; lit_01's 1265 and this item's 1990 are both 'old', which is the point.

prompt: In conversazione: 'Stamattina _____ tardi.' Choose: mi alzai / mi sono alzato.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo in speech (mi sono alzato)

explanation: 'Stamattina mi sono alzato tardi' means 'This morning I got up late.' A this-morning event mentioned in conversation is the passato prossimo's core territory: recent, spoken, and still bearing on how the day is going. Mi alzai would be jarring here, the register mismatch at its sharpest.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: In un messaggio a un amico: 'Domenica scorsa _____ al mare.' Choose: andammo / siamo andati.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo in informal writing (siamo andati)

explanation: 'Domenica scorsa siamo andati al mare' means 'Last Sunday we went to the seaside.' A message to a friend is writing, but it is casual writing, and the passato prossimo follows the register rather than the medium. Writing alone does not summon the remoto: what would is narrative or historical prose. Andammo in a text message would read as a joke.
examiner_note: Deliberately tests that the remoto tracks register, not the written/spoken split. Informal writing is the case that breaks the naive 'written = remoto' rule.

prompt: In conversazione: 'Che cosa _____ ieri sera?' Choose: faceste / avete fatto.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo in speech (avete fatto)

explanation: 'Che cosa avete fatto ieri sera?' means 'What did you do last night?' A question put directly to someone in conversation is spoken register, so it takes the passato prossimo. Questions are worth noticing separately: the remoto is a narrator's tense, and a narrator does not ask the listener anything, so the remoto is especially odd in a direct question.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: In conversazione: 'L'estate scorsa _____ una settimana in Grecia.' Choose: passammo / abbiamo passato.

markpoints.label: Passato prossimo in speech (abbiamo passato)

explanation: 'L'estate scorsa abbiamo passato una settimana in Grecia' means 'Last summer we spent a week in Greece.' Recounting a holiday to someone is conversation, so the passato prossimo carries it. The remoto passammo would push the trip into the tone of a memoir, which is a register the situation does not call for.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: Complete: 'Da bambino, ogni estate _____ dai nonni in campagna.' Choose: vado / andavo.

markpoints.label: Imperfect for a past routine (andavo)

explanation: 'Da bambino, ogni estate andavo dai nonni in campagna' means 'As a child, every summer I used to go to my grandparents' in the countryside.' Ogni estate marks a routine, and the imperfect indicative is the tense for a routine, but that alone does not settle it: the routine also has to be past. Da bambino does that, sealing the summers in childhood. Vado would claim you still go.
examiner_note: Tense-choice diagnostic: single markpoint, no formation buckets fire. The supplied wrong form is the only plausible attempt and is word-anchored per criterion 18. candidate_tenses surfaces post-answer only.

prompt: Complete: 'Adesso _____ in palestra tre volte alla settimana.' Choose: vado / andavo.

markpoints.label: Present for a current routine (vado)

explanation: 'Adesso vado in palestra tre volte alla settimana' means 'These days I go to the gym three times a week.' Tre volte alla settimana marks a routine just as ogni estate does, so habit alone cannot be the cue. What decides it is the frame: adesso puts the routine in present time, and a present routine takes the present indicative. Andavo would file the gym habit away as something you have given up.
examiner_note: Same-surface pair with tc_pres_imp_hab_01 (house technique 3): identical verb and supplied forms, opposite answers. The pair is what makes the weak diagnostic teachable: it isolates the frame adverb as the only conditioning factor.

prompt: Complete: 'Il postino ogni giorno _____ di qui verso le dieci; è così da anni.' Choose: passa / passava.

markpoints.label: Present for a current routine (passa)

explanation: 'Il postino ogni giorno passa di qui verso le dieci' means 'The postman comes by here every day around ten.' Ogni giorno gives the routine, and è così da anni tells you the routine is still running, which puts the frame in present time and selects the present indicative. Passava would move the postman's round into a past that the second clause says has not ended.
examiner_note: Criterion 18, rightward extension: 'passa' is a substring of 'passava', so the any_phrase is word-anchored or a learner typing the wrong form would be credited. This is the brief's own worked example of the exposure.

prompt: Complete: 'Quando abitavamo in centro, il postino _____ di qui verso le dieci.' Choose: passa / passava.

markpoints.label: Imperfect for a past routine (passava)

explanation: 'Quando abitavamo in centro, il postino passava di qui verso le dieci' means 'When we lived in the town centre, the postman used to come by here around ten.' The routine is the same as before, but quando abitavamo in centro seals it inside a past stretch you have since left, and a past routine takes the imperfect indicative. The neighbouring abitavamo is your cue: the frame verb and the routine verb sit in the same tense.
examiner_note: Same-surface pair with tc_pres_imp_hab_03 (house technique 3), using the bucket's own canonical_example. Criterion 18, third direction: 'passa' is a substring of the correct 'passava', so the must_not_include entry is word-anchored.
